task_completion.description: Load the flat-field FITS frames into AstroImageJ
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ interface open showing an image stack loaded from the ~/AstroImages/flat_analysis/flats/ directory.

task_completion.description: Generate and save the median Z-projection
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ Z-Project window with 'Median' projection type selected, or an image window showing the median projection being saved as median_flat.fits in the results directory.

task_completion.description: Generate and save the standard deviation Z-projection
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ Z-Project window with 'Standard Deviation' projection type selected, or an image window showing the stddev projection being saved as stddev_flat.fits in the results directory.

task_completion.description: Measure median signal and noise for four quadrants
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ Results table or measurement windows showing median values extracted from rectangular ROI selections (representing quadrants) on both the median and standard deviation projection images.

task_completion.description: Identify bad pixels based on standard deviation thresholds
task_completion.visual_evidence: Use of AstroImageJ tools (such as Image Math, Thresholding, Histogram, or Macros) on the standard deviation projection to find pixels >5x or <0.1x the overall median standard deviation.

task_completion.description: Export the final analysis to noise_analysis.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of noise_analysis.txt with exactly 5 lines matching the requested format (Median signal, Median noise, GAIN_ESTIMATE, BAD_PIXEL_COUNT, POISSON_CHECK) populated with calculated values.

integrity.description: Agent used AstroImageJ GUI for processing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the AstroImageJ graphical interface (menus, dialogs, image windows) rather than bypassing it with Python scripts or command-line ImageMagick tools.

integrity.description: Results file contains actual calculated numbers
integrity.visual_evidence: The noise_analysis.txt file must contain actual numerical values for signals, noise, gain, and bad pixel counts, not the literal bracketed placeholder text (e.g., '[Median signal Q1]') from the prompt.

integrity.description: FITS files were genuinely generated and saved
integrity.visual_evidence: The median_flat.fits and stddev_flat.fits files should be saved via AstroImageJ's save functionality, not just created as empty files using the 'touch' command in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real cryogenically cooled flat-field CCD frames from the Palomar 200-inch Hale Telescope's Large Format Camera (LFC). The expected output directory is /home/ga/AstroImages/flat_analysis/results/ and must contain exactly three files: median_flat.fits, stddev_flat.fits, and noise_analysis.txt.
